A few seasons worth talking about

Placeholder case studies — swap these for real collections, launches, or projects with metrics that matter to you.

Reliance Trends

Kidswear Design Calendar Overhaul

Owned end-to-end design calendar execution and product development for kidswear, aligning trend research with commercial targets across every drop.

Result — conversion rate up from 40% to 58%
Reliance Trends

Sell-Through Optimization

Rebuilt trims, sourcing and vendor coordination workflows to keep full-price and discounted sell-through consistently high, season after season.

Result — 85% full-price, 90% discounted sell-through
Splash · Landmark Group

Youth Apparel & Licensing Collections

Designed high-value youth fashion and licensing-brand collections in Dubai, translating market research into product stories with strong customer loyalty.

Result — high-street aligned graphics & styles
Career path

Ten years, six brands, one throughline

From formalwear to kidswear to digital product — the throughline is always systems behind the aesthetics.

2019 — Present

Design Manager, Kidswear — Reliance Trends

Lead end-to-end design calendar execution and product development. Mentor a cross-functional team, drive quarterly special projects, and manage vendor coordination, sampling, trims and sourcing.

2016 — 2019

Designer, Youth Apparel & Accessories — Splash (Landmark Group), Dubai

Designed high-value fashion collections for youth men/women and licensing brands; conducted market research and built customer loyalty through high-street aligned styling.

2015 — 2016

Designer, Ajile — Aditya Birla Fashion & Retail (Pantaloons)

Conceptualized men's athleisurewear — graphics, tech packs and fabric stories for seasonal drops.

2014 — 2015

Designer, Youthwear/Jeanswear — Arvind Brands

Coordinated with merchandising and production teams to roll out trend-aligned lines.

2012 — 2014

Lead Designer, Menswear — Inkfruit.com

Created graphic-driven apparel for a fast-growing digital fashion brand, delivering high-performing SKUs through pop culture and visual storytelling.

2011 — 2012

Designer, Formalwear — Reid & Taylor

Designed business and lifestyle formals including suits, shirts and trousers.

2024

Executive PG, UX & Interface Design

IIT Roorkee

2011

B.Des, Fashion & Apparel

NIFT Hyderabad

How it actually works

The process behind every collection

Six things I own in parallel, every season. Tap through to see how each one runs.

01 — Research & Forecasting

Starting from what's actually coming, not what's trending on a mood board.

Every collection starts months out — reading trend services, competitor sets, and consumer signal before a single sketch happens.

WGSNPinterest boardsMiroGoogle Trends
01

Trend & market scanning

Color, silhouette, fabric direction pulled from forecasting services and competitor releases.

02

Consumer insight gathering

Voice-of-customer, returns feedback and social listening folded into the seasonal brief.

03

Mood boards & concept development

Turning scattered references into a coherent point of view the whole team can design against.

02 — Collection Design

Turning a concept into a sellable, makeable line.

Line planning, product innovation and sampling — where the ideas meet fabric, cost, and a factory calendar.

Adobe IllustratorPLM softwareFigma
01

Line & SKU planning

Architecting the collection across category, price tier, and silhouette mix.

02

Product innovation

New materials, construction techniques, and sustainability improvements pushed into the range.

03

Sampling & fit iteration

Tech packs, fit sessions, and revisions managed through to sample approval.

03 — Data & Analytics

Letting last season's numbers shape this season's brief.

Sell-through, returns, and margin data reviewed before design decisions get locked, not after.

LookerExcel / SheetsTableau
01

Sales performance review

Bestseller and laggard analysis feeding directly into next season's line plan.

02

Returns & fit analytics

Return-reason data used to fix recurring fit or quality issues at the source.

03

Post-launch reporting

Closing the loop — what sold, why, and what carries forward to the next collection.

04 — Coordination

Design doesn't ship alone — it ships through six other teams.

Keeping design, merchandising, production and marketing pulling in the same direction, on the same calendar.

SlackNotionGoogle Meet
01

Cross-team alignment

Regular syncs across merchandising, production and marketing to keep the collection story consistent.

02

Vendor & factory coordination

Sampling feasibility and production timelines negotiated directly with manufacturing partners.

03

Leadership presentations

Building the case for the collection and getting stakeholder buy-in before go-ahead.

05 — Team & Timelines

Running a studio calendar without missing a season.

Weekly, monthly and seasonal milestones, and a team that knows exactly what's due and when.

AsanaNotion calendarsAirtable
01

Seasonal calendar management

Critical-path milestones mapped from concept to delivery, season over season.

02

Team management

Workload distribution, mentoring, and keeping a design team unblocked.

03

Deadline tracking

A single source of truth for what's due, who owns it, and what's at risk.

06 — Systems

The stack that keeps all of the above from falling apart.

A design career runs on more than design software — here's the full system, mapped to what it's actually for.

See the full stack below ↓
01

Design & PLM

Illustrator, Figma and a PLM system for tech packs and sample tracking.

02

Planning & comms

Asana or Notion for calendars, Slack for the day-to-day coordination noise.

03

Data & reporting

Looker or Sheets to keep the design brief honest against real sales numbers.

The stack

SaaS tools I run the studio on

Research & UX

Design

  • Photoshop — visualization
  • 3D Design — product renders
Collaboration

  • AI-led tools — design acceleration
  • Slack — team coordination
Strategy
  • Design strategy & trend forecasting
  • Team leadership & mentoring
  • User research & visual communication
Outside the studio

Long-distance runner competing in ultras and marathons · Tabla player · Published author, showcased at major book fairs
